chunk_summary: Guidance on essential fatty acid (EFA) targets for childhood growth and neural development, emphasizing omega-6 (LA), omega-3 (ALA), and vegetarian DHA sources, with practical dietary advice for Indian diets.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of omega-6 (LA) and omega-3 (ALA) in childhood growth and neural development.
Identify age-specific EFA intake targets and translate them into practical dietary recommendations.
Recognize vegetarian DHA sources and supplementation needs in specific populations.
Assess household dietary patterns to recommend feasible EFA improvements.
key_facts: Adequate Intake (AI) for ages 1–3 years: LA ~7 g/day, ALA ~0.7 g/day; ages 4–8 years: LA ~10 g/day, ALA ~0.9 g/day.
Vegetarian DHA sources include algae-based supplements, suitable for families avoiding fish and eggs.
Indian dietary sources: sunflower, safflower, soybean, and groundnut oils for LA; mustard oil, flaxseed, chia, and walnuts for ALA.
Mixed oils and whole food sources are preferred over reliance on a single oil.
Supplementation is advised for children with very low DHA intake or higher neurodevelopmental risk (e.g., preterm, LBW, neurodisability).
Specialist guidance is required for omega-3 decisions in fat malabsorption or cholestasis.
reference_quote: DRI Adequate Intake anchors are practical for teaching: ages 1–3 years LA about 7 g/day and ALA about 0.7 g/day; ages 4–8 years LA about 10 g/day and ALA about 0.9 g/day.
document_key_topics: Essential fatty acid (EFA) intake targets for children
Special considerations for fat malabsorption and neurodevelopmental risk
